what_it_does: Creates the shared-channel meeting ground in Supabase: public.channels (registry mirroring agent_sessions/channels.jsonl — id/kind/name/purpose/mode/coordinator/status/members jsonb/origin jsonb/posts/shared boolean/timestamps) and public.channel_posts (id uuid, channel_id FK cascade, from_session, sender_kind session|client, to_session nullable, thread, kind, text, created_at) with indexes on channel/thread/sender. Adds a plpgsql trigger _bump_channel_on_post that recounts posts and updates last_activity on each insert, adds both tables to the supabase_realtime publication (guarded against duplicate), enables RLS on both, grants ALL to service_role+postgres and SELECT to authenticated (no anon), and ensures pgcrypto for gen_random_uuid.
observations: shared boolean defaults false: a channel is internal (never leaves the box) unless explicitly opted in.
Two-level visibility documented: shared=true AND the channel listed in the client's clients.channels row.
sender_kind tags origin (session vs client) so the company injector skips self-origin posts and tags Claude Design's posts.
posts is denormalized and kept current by an AFTER INSERT trigger that does a full COUNT(*) per insert.
Realtime publication add is wrapped in a do-block catching duplicate_table for idempotency.
Explicit NO anon SELECT with C-1 rationale: a broad using(true) anon policy would OR-widen past the scoped 0005/0006 policies.
Schema is declared PROVISIONAL pending 'fork' confirm/adjust; mirrors the mail-leaf post shape (frm/to/thread/text/ts).
standouts: Zero-box-exposure participation model: both sides reach OUTBOUND to Supabase as the neutral room; nobody connects into Tim's machine.
Opt-in, fail-closed channel visibility with two independent gates (shared flag + per-client channel list).
The OR-widening warning (C-1) showing awareness that an over-broad RLS policy silently defeats scoped ones.
conventions: Idempotent create-if-not-exists, drop-trigger-if-exists, duplicate-guarded publication add.
RLS enabled but policies deferred to 0005/0006 (this file grants privileges, those scope rows).
Mirrors existing company fabric shapes (channels.jsonl, mail-leaf posts) for clean sync.
create extension if not exists pgcrypto appears at file end (also repeated in 0004).
concerns: The bump trigger does a full COUNT(*) of channel_posts on every insert — O(n) per post, a write-amplification cost on busy channels.
kind/mode/status/sender_kind are free-text with conventions only in comments (no CHECK/enum).
RLS is enabled here but no policy is created in this file; rows are inaccessible to authenticated until 0005/0006 run (correct only if those always follow).
notes: The shared channel registry + posts tables that make Supabase the neutral outbound meeting room between the company fabric and Claude Design, opt-in/fail-closed, Realtime-published, with a post-count trigger.
questions: Has 'fork' confirmed the provisional schema, or could the shape still change?
Why recount posts fully in the trigger instead of incrementing posts = posts + 1?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: stated as channels-as-shared-meeting-ground; the DDL creates the registry+posts, realtime publication, and RLS scaffolding that implement the described participation model.
apparent_themes: neutral shared room with zero inbound box exposure
mirroring existing fabric shapes for clean sync
intention_signals: Let Claude Design and company sessions converse on the same data through Supabase as a neutral outbound rendezvous, with visibility off by default and Realtime carrying each side's posts to the other.
novelty: The both-sides-reach-outbound, nobody-reaches-in participation model and the two-gate (shared flag + per-client list) opt-in visibility encoded across schema and RLS plan.
contribution: Brings the cross-party messaging substrate — the channel registry, post log, realtime publication, and bump trigger that carry the company-fabric/Claude-Design conversation on shared Supabase rows.
summary_for_embedding: SQL migration creating channels + channel_posts tables (mirroring the company fabric), a post-count trigger, realtime publication, and RLS scaffolding for a Supabase shared meeting ground.
intention_for_embedding: To make Supabase a neutral outbound shared room where the company fabric and Claude Design exchange posts on the same opt-in, fail-closed, realtime-delivered rows.
